title: Abstract concepts, language and sociality: from acquisition to inner speech.

publication.journal: Philos Trans R Soc Lond B Biol Sci

assets.abstract: The problem of representation of abstract concepts, such as 'freedom' and 'justice', has become particularly crucial in recent years, owing to the increased success of embodied and grounded views of cognition. We will present a novel view on abstract concepts and abstract words. Since abstract concepts do not have single objects as referents, children and adults might rely more on input from others to learn them; we, therefore, suggest that linguistic and social experience play an important role

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Language as mantra — mantra meditation, inner speech, focused attention, verbal repetition.
